svg.text: 14.13 · The anti-pattern catalog — mistake → fix (grouped by module)
the recurring journey mistakes · ⚠ = a money-never-lies violation
MODELING / TYPES (M01–M03)
⚠ FLOAT/DOUBLE for money → DECIMAL / integer minor units
UUIDv4 clustered PK → ULID/UUIDv7/Snowflake (time-ordered)
UUID as CHAR(36) → BINARY(16)
reserved word `transaction` → transaction_
(money type is the #1 fintech foundation, M03)
INDEXING / QUERIES (M05/M06)
SELECT * → only needed columns (enables covering)
N+1 queries → a join · leading wildcard LIKE '%x' → restructure
function on column (DATE(col)=) → range · implicit conversion → match types
missing index on hot filter · over-indexing a write-heavy table
verify every index with EXPLAIN (M06)
TRANSACTIONS / LOCKING (M07/M08)
long-running transactions → keep short (HLL bloat, contention)
inconsistent lock ordering → canonical order (deadlocks)
naive DDL on a huge table → online DDL (M13/13.6)
DDL during long queries → MDL stall (everything freezes)
DURABILITY / REPLICATION (M09/M10)
⚠ flush_log=2 / sync_binlog=0 for money → 1/1 (full durability)
STATEMENT binlog → ROW (no divergence)
⚠ money-decision read off a lagging replica → primary (double-spend)
failover without fencing → split-brain (forked ledger)
DISTRIBUTED (M12)
⚠ dual-write (DB + queue) → outbox / CDC
⚠ no idempotency on retryable ops → idempotency keys
cross-shard 2PC/XA → Saga
⚠ eventual consistency for money without reconciliation → reconcile
OPERATIONS (M13)
⚠ untested backups → tested restore drills + reconciliation
no early-warning monitoring → watch lag/HLL/checkpoint/disk/semi-sync
cargo-cult config → tune the few that matter, measure
shared superuser / root in apps → least privilege
the ⚠ money-critical anti-patterns ARE the money-never-lies violations — this is the checklist against them
FLOAT money · stale-replica money read · weak durability config · dual-write · no idempotency · untested backup · no reconciliation
